title: Listening - Part 3: Taking Part in a Sporting Event
description: You are going to hear five extracts in which people talk about why they decided to take part in a sporting event. For questions 19–23, choose from the list A–H the main reason each speaker gives for participating. Use the letters once only. There are three letters that you do not need to use.
instructions: You are going to hear five extracts in which people talk about why they decided to take part in a sporting event. For questions 19–23, choose from the list A–H the main reason each speaker gives for participating. Use the letters once only. There are three letters that you do not need to use.

extracts.context: Five people talk about taking part in a sporting event.
extracts.audio_script: Speaker 1 Woman: I’d never imagined myself running ten kilometres. Then a local charity helped my neighbour adapt her home after an accident, and I saw what a difference that made. [19]When they advertised a sponsored run, I entered because I wanted my friends and relatives to donate money to support the same organisation.[/19] Training was harder than expected, especially in the rain, but remembering why I was doing it kept me going. I completed the course slowly, yet collected more than I’d hoped.||Speaker 2 Man: I regularly cycle to work, but I’d never attempted a long-distance ride. When I saw an event covering eighty kilometres, I nearly ignored it because I knew nobody else taking part. [20]Eventually I entered simply to find out whether I had the determination to prepare properly and complete a distance that seemed almost impossible at first.[/20] Crossing the finish line mattered far more than my position. I’m already looking for something slightly harder to try next year.||Speaker 3 Woman: My friend Maya is keen on swimming and wanted to enter a lake-swimming event, but she felt nervous about doing it alone. I prefer team games and wouldn’t normally choose cold open water. [21]I agreed to enter because it gave us a goal to work towards together and meant Maya would have someone she knew beside her on the day.[/21] The training sessions were actually great fun. I’m not sure I’ll repeat the race, but I’m glad we shared the experience.||Speaker 4 Man: A walking event was organised in a national park several hours from where I live. Friends thought it was strange to travel so far just to walk, since there are plenty of routes nearby. [22]But I entered because the course passed through mountains and lakes I’d only seen in photographs, and I wanted an excuse to explore that part of the country.[/22] The final climb was exhausting, but the views were worth every step. I’m planning to return without a time limit.||Speaker 5 Woman: Each December, my father and older sisters take part in the town’s winter fun run wearing ridiculous costumes. When I was younger, I watched from the pavement and handed them drinks. This year I was finally old enough to join. [23]I didn’t enter because I enjoy running particularly; I wanted to take my place in an event my family has done together for as long as I can remember.[/23] We all finished together, dressed as snowmen, which was exactly as embarrassing and enjoyable as I’d expected.

dialogue.speaker: speaker_1
dialogue.text: I’d never imagined myself running ten kilometres. Then a local charity helped my neighbour adapt her home after an accident, and I saw what a difference that made. When they advertised a sponsored run, I entered because I wanted my friends and relatives to donate money to support the same organisation. Training was harder than expected, especially in the rain, but remembering why I was doing it kept me going. I completed the course slowly, yet collected more than I’d hoped.

dialogue.speaker: speaker_2
dialogue.text: I regularly cycle to work, but I’d never attempted a long-distance ride. When I saw an event covering eighty kilometres, I nearly ignored it because I knew nobody else taking part. Eventually I entered simply to find out whether I had the determination to prepare properly and complete a distance that seemed almost impossible at first. Crossing the finish line mattered far more than my position. I’m already looking for something slightly harder to try next year.

dialogue.speaker: speaker_3
dialogue.text: My friend Maya is keen on swimming and wanted to enter a lake-swimming event, but she felt nervous about doing it alone. I prefer team games and wouldn’t normally choose cold open water. I agreed to enter because it gave us a goal to work towards together and meant Maya would have someone she knew beside her on the day. The training sessions were actually great fun. I’m not sure I’ll repeat the race, but I’m glad we shared the experience.

dialogue.speaker: speaker_4
dialogue.text: A walking event was organised in a national park several hours from where I live. Friends thought it was strange to travel so far just to walk, since there are plenty of routes nearby. But I entered because the course passed through mountains and lakes I’d only seen in photographs, and I wanted an excuse to explore that part of the country. The final climb was exhausting, but the views were worth every step. I’m planning to return without a time limit.

dialogue.speaker: speaker_5
dialogue.text: Each December, my father and older sisters take part in the town’s winter fun run wearing ridiculous costumes. When I was younger, I watched from the pavement and handed them drinks. This year I was finally old enough to join. I didn’t enter because I enjoy running particularly; I wanted to take my place in an event my family has done together for as long as I can remember. We all finished together, dressed as snowmen, which was exactly as embarrassing and enjoyable as I’d expected.

options.text: to raise money for an organisation
to set themselves a personal challenge
to spend time with a friend

questions.speaker: Speaker 1
questions.explanation: The speaker joined the sponsored run in order to collect money for a helpful local charity.

questions.speaker: Speaker 2
questions.explanation: The speaker entered the long ride to test her own ability and determination.

questions.speaker: Speaker 3
questions.explanation: The speaker participated mainly so that a friend would have company during preparation and the event.

questions.speaker: Speaker 4
questions.explanation: The speaker was drawn to the event as a way to explore attractive countryside she did not know.

questions.speaker: Speaker 5
questions.explanation: The speaker wanted to join a family activity that has taken place for many years.
